title: Turkish-Style Manti
description: Delicate Turkish dumplings filled with a sweet and savory mixture of ground meat and spices, served with a side of gooey cheese and fragrant herbs.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, parsley, manti spice mix, and salt. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
2. Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
3. Divide the mixture into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
4. Roll each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
5. Brush the edges of the dough with a little water.
6. Place a tablespoon of the meat mixture in the center of each dough circle.
7. Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
8. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the manti for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
9. While the manti are cooking, melt the gooey cheese in a small saucepan over low heat.
10. Serve the manti hot with the melted cheese and a sprinkle of fragrant herbs.
